title: ctenopteris pseudocapillaris
intro: Ctenopteris pseudocapillaris, also known as the Australian maidenhair fern, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the family Blechnaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Ctenopteris pseudocapillaris include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as providing antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or topical creams, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
